data.title: GCL Global Holdings Switches to EY Singapore as SEC Auditor
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

GCL Global Holdings ( (GCL) ) has issued an announcement.

GCL Global Holdings Ltd, a Singapore-based foreign private issuer with SEC-reporting obligations, prepares consolidated financial statements under U.S. GAAP and operates within a cross-border regulatory framework. Its governance structure includes an audit committee that oversees the appointment and evaluation of independent external auditors for its U.S. filings.

On March 24, 2026, GCL Global Holdings’ board dismissed Marcum Asia CPAs LLP and appointed Ernst & Young LLP Singapore as its new independent registered public accounting firm for audits of financial statements to be filed with the SEC. The company reported no disagreements with Marcum Asia over its March 31, 2024 and 2025 audits, though prior material weaknesses in internal controls were noted, and Marcum Asia later confirmed its agreement with the company’s description of the auditor transition in a March 26, 2026 letter to the SEC.

The most recent analyst rating on (GCL) stock is a Hold with a $0.80 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GCL Global Holdings stock, see the GCL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GCL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GCL is a Neutral.

The score is primarily driven by mixed financial performance: strong growth and improved profitability are offset by negative operating/free cash flow and higher leverage. Technicals add pressure as the stock trades below major moving averages with bearish momentum indicators. Valuation contributes modestly negatively due to an uninformative negative P/E and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on GCL stock, click here.

More about GCL Global Holdings

GCL Global Holdings Ltd is a Singapore-based foreign private issuer listed in the United States, reporting to the SEC under Form 20-F requirements. The company prepares consolidated financial statements in accordance with U.S. GAAP and SEC standards, indicating a focus on internationally oriented operations and cross-border capital markets access.

data.title: Red Metal to Return Capital via In Specie Distribution of Maronan Metals Shares
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Red Metal Limited ( (AU:RDM) ).

Red Metal Limited has notified the ASX of a return of capital to shareholders via an in specie distribution of securities in Maronan Metals Limited, which is already listed on the exchange. The transaction applies to Red Metal’s fully paid ordinary shares and is structured as a non-selective capital reduction, meaning all eligible shareholders will participate.

By distributing Maronan Metals shares directly, Red Metal is effectively transferring part of its investment in the related entity back to its investors, altering the composition of its balance sheet while maintaining shareholder exposure to Maronan’s prospects. The move may refine Red Metal’s capital structure and portfolio focus, while giving shareholders direct holdings in Maronan, potentially improving transparency and liquidity for investors in both companies.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RDM) stock is a Hold with a A$0.12 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Red Metal Limited stock, see the AU:RDM Stock Forecast page.

More about Red Metal Limited

Red Metal Limited is an Australian-listed resources company trading under the ASX code RDM, with a focus on mineral exploration and development. Its operations are structured around equity interests in other listed entities, including Maronan Metals Limited (ASX: MMA), which provides exposure to additional exploration and project development opportunities in the mining sector.

data.title: Enegex Confirms Broad Gold System in Côte d’Ivoire with Strong Maiden Aircore Results
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Enegex Limited ( (AU:ENX) ) just unveiled an update.

Enegex Limited has reported strong maiden aircore drilling results from the Kalama Bave prospect within its Tougbe exploration permit in Côte d’Ivoire, confirming multiple shallow gold-mineralised zones in weathered chlorite schist. The program, comprising 94 shallow holes to an average depth of 30 metres, showed that around half of the holes contained reportable gold intercepts, including several high-grade intervals from surface or near-surface.

The drilling has validated earlier auger anomalies and demonstrated that the gold system is broad and, in places, open to the limits of current drilling, prompting plans for additional aircore drilling in the coming weeks to extend coverage. Enegex also plans deeper reverse circulation drilling to test the mineralised zones in fresh rock below 25 metres, which could significantly advance resource delineation and enhance the company’s positioning in a previously undrilled gold district of Côte d’Ivoire.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:ENX) stock is a Sell with a A$0.19 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Enegex Limited stock, see the AU:ENX Stock Forecast page.

More about Enegex Limited

Enegex Limited is an ASX-listed mineral exploration company focused on gold exploration, currently advancing projects in Côte d’Ivoire. The company is targeting greenfield prospects within the Tougbe-Gogo permit group, including the Kalama Bave prospect, where it is using auger, aircore and reverse circulation drilling to delineate new gold mineralised systems.

Average Trading Volume: 187,017

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: RMI Maps Priority Gold Targets at Saudi Shaib Marqan Project
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Resource Mining Corporation Limited ( (AU:RMI) ) has provided an update.

Resource Minerals International has completed a high-resolution ground magnetic survey over 91.88 square kilometres at its Shaib Marqan gold project in Saudi Arabia’s Arabian Shield, marking the first phase of systematic work on the licence. The survey defines seven major fault systems and multiple structural corridors considered highly prospective for gold, building on earlier rock sample assays of up to 40.35 grams per tonne.

The results have allowed the company to delineate four priority target zones, labelled Z1 to Z4, that will be the focus of immediate follow-up exploration and drilling. RMI plans to begin rock chip sampling over these zones shortly, followed by an initial 5,000-metre drilling program, a step that could materially advance the project’s evaluation and strengthen the company’s position within Saudi Arabia’s emerging gold sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RMI) stock is a Hold with a A$0.03 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Resource Mining Corporation Limited stock, see the AU:RMI Stock Forecast page.

More about Resource Mining Corporation Limited

Resource Minerals International Ltd is an ASX-listed exploration company focused on gold and base metals, with a growing portfolio in Saudi Arabia’s Arabian Shield. Its primary assets include the Shaib Marqan and Wadi Salamah projects, targeting structurally controlled gold and associated mineral systems in underexplored terranes.

Average Trading Volume: 573,793

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$24.71M

data.title: Great Western to Start Fully Funded Nine-Target Copper-Gold Drilling Push in WA
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Great Western Exploration Limited ( (AU:GTE) ) has shared an announcement.

Great Western Exploration will launch an aggressive copper-gold drilling campaign in Western Australia, testing nine fully funded targets within its Yerrida Basin projects near Meekatharra. Backed by a recent $3.75 million capital raising and a cash balance of $4.3 million, the company is positioned to advance multiple prospects in a region known for significant VHMS copper-gold deposits.

Drilling is scheduled to begin in mid-April at the Oval target, interpreted as the potential core of a VHMS system analogous to Sandfire Resources’ DeGrussa deposit. Subsequent programs will test the Diorama, Juggernaut and Baroo copper-gold targets, while regional prospects are progressed to drill-ready status, potentially reshaping the company’s exploration pipeline and value outlook if successful.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:GTE) stock is a Hold with a A$0.02 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Great Western Exploration Limited stock, see the AU:GTE Stock Forecast page.

More about Great Western Exploration Limited

Great Western Exploration Limited is an Australian mineral exploration company focused on discovering copper and gold deposits in Western Australia. The company holds an extensive land position in the Yerrida Basin near Meekatharra, targeting volcanic-hosted massive sulphide style copper-gold systems similar to the nearby DeGrussa and Monty deposits.

Vox Royalty ( (TSE:VOXR) ) has provided an announcement.

Vox Royalty reported record 2025 results, with royalty and net precious metal receipts climbing to $16.6 million and gross profit rising to $10.1 million, driven by a commodity mix led by gold and supported by copper and iron ore. The company also delivered record operating cash flow and adjusted EBITDA for both the full year and fourth quarter, reflecting strong organic growth across its portfolio and increased exposure to cash-flowing assets.

Management highlighted early 2026 milestones including an amendment to its second Greenstone offtake stream, new Australian royalty acquisitions, full repayment of its revolving credit facility, and inclusion in a major junior gold miners index. These developments, combined with a strengthened balance sheet and scalable model, position Vox for enhanced operating leverage and underscore its improving profile among institutional and index investors.

data.title: Village Farms Cuts Loan Cost and Extends FCC Financing to 2031
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Village Farms International ( (VFF) ) has shared an announcement.

Village Farms International has secured improved terms on its long‑standing loan with Farm Credit Canada, cutting the interest rate by 50 basis points and extending the maturity to February 2031 on a US$15.4 million facility. The loan now carries a variable rate below 7.0%, with all other key conditions unchanged, giving the company more favorable financing and longer runway to support its growth plans.

Management framed the amendment as a sign of both lender confidence and the strengthening of Village Farms’ business, which it expects to continue expanding in 2026. The enhanced loan structure should bolster liquidity and financial flexibility as the company pursues its cannabis, plant-based products and clean energy strategies across Canada, Europe and the United States, potentially reinforcing its competitive standing in these markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (VFF) stock is a Hold with a $2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Village Farms International stock, see the VFF Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VFF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VFF is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak cash flow conversion and contracting revenue (financial performance) and by soft technical momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD). These are partially offset by a constructive earnings-call outlook highlighting record profitability, strong operational execution, and capacity-driven growth initiatives.

To see Spark’s full report on VFF stock, click here.

More about Village Farms International

Village Farms International, Inc. is a global player in cannabis, plant-based consumer packaged goods and sustainable innovation, leveraging decades of controlled-environment agriculture and Dutch farming expertise. The company operates more than seven million square feet of greenhouse and indoor cultivation assets, making it one of the largest and most profitable cannabis operators worldwide.

In Canada, Village Farms runs one of the largest EU‑GMP certified cannabis facilities from its Delta, British Columbia campus and exports to international medical markets while holding leading market share in dried flower formats. It also owns a broad Canadian brand portfolio, is one of only ten licensed cannabis operators in the Netherlands, runs the CBDistillery hemp‑derived wellness brand in the U.S., operates a Clean Energy division converting landfill gas to renewable natural gas, and holds an equity stake in Vanguard Food LP.
